Don't get me wrong, it's a fun game. The problem is that it lacks overall content, and a lot of the content is locked behind DLC paywalls. You really only have 2 options for things to do if you're playing singleplayer, which can get pretty repetitive quite quickly, especially when considering the lack of content without DLCs. A game with good potential and is still fun for a short while, but sadly locked behind a paywall and an almost requirement to play multiplayer.
Very great game. I like the customization. Buuuuttttt i dislike the DLCs. They overuse the DLCs so... Othervise i like it. Extremly recommended

Amazing game and has lots of things to do.
personally I only really play sandbox and even just that is so fun with friends,
I'm not even a good builder or anything but you can make practically anything
and my laptop usually turns into a nuclear reactor when trying to run most games but this runs perfectly fine.
Only real problem with the game is how many DLC's there are and how pricey some of them can be...
Dear god, the horrors you can create in this amazing game are endless.

Starting off, the game itself is fun. The building is easy to learn, hard to master. I had quite a lot of expirience with vehicle building games, I have 400 hours in from the depths and a lot in plane crazy. I had no issues with any performance while running it on an average gaming laptop. This game has both sandbox and pve campaign modes. One free campaign is incredibly boring, the other is fun but a bit short. 

My main issue is how all customization, some parts and TWO ENTIRE CAMPAIGNS are locked behind dlc paywalls. Thats right, want a good looking tail rotor? DLC. Flamethrower? DLC. Any decorative parts? DLC. An interesting campaign to play? DLC. Mabie a texture for your craft? Yeah, we have a DLC for that. After helldivers 2 monetization of paid games just pisses me off. Why is new content behind paywalls? Look, If you want a vehicle building game, there are better options.
DLC is overpriced and limits the creativity a lot but the game is fun in itself, though i would reccomend making DLCs less pricy, but overall i reccomend this game

This is the kind of game where you are given a massive selection of various blocks, motorized parts, wheels, etc. and are told "go wild". The only limitation to what kind of machines you create are your imagination and whether or not you can make them work. And trust me, if you're willing to tinker and hone your design enough you CAN make it work. There are people who have literally made fully functional DOOM arcade machines, it's insane what you can achieve. Want to build a submarine that can hover on land and transforms into a helicopter? Absolutely. Wanna make a pickup truck that can fly in space? Yes sir, it's actually fairly easy to do. Want a realistic CIWS automated defense system complete with on-board laser targeting system? Believe it or not, YES.

Gameplay aside, I really like how active the developers are with their community. Frequent build challenges give the players a chance to show off their best work (and even a chance to be featured in the main menu as background footage!) and dev vlogs show off upcoming content, allowing players to see the new parts in action, get technical info about them, etc. Furthermore, features that get highly requested will often make it into the game, provided the team doesn't hit a roadblock with technical limitations. For example, the community has wanted tank tracks for YEARS, and it took the team 5 tries over the years to add them and they had to LITERALLY reinvent the wheel to make it happen, but make it happen they did. I'm not joking by the way, they had to rebuild the wheel system from the ground up to add them, and every wheel in the game benefited from it. But, here's that technical limitation I mentioned: people wanted *modular* tracks, but unfortunately the build menu can't do that, blocks have no way of linking up to each other like that. Each segment of the track would be its own block, which could cause problems on bigger builds. Just don't ask for a 1x1x1 cube. They staunchly refuse to add it.

Now, the game isn't perfect, so I do want to mention this:
They do often release DLC. Sometimes it might just be a few blocks and some cosmetics, other times it might just be player outfits, and sometimes it's an entire map/campaign. The game itself has a nice lineup of sandbox maps as well as 2 "campaigns", so to speak. Stranded is the much older of the 2, and is fairly simple in its style: You have a limited array of parts to build a vehicle with and you have to collect various scrap chunks and bring them to checkpoints to unlock new parts and eventually build an escape rocket to get home. No enemies, no mission system, just the challenge of collecting things in increasingly difficult locations.

Pioneers is the much newer one, and it is an ACTUAL campaign: unlock blocks through finding crates, harvest materials to actually CRAFT the blocks and increase the amount of them in your inventory (Stranded doesn't use this, you just have infinite uses of a block on any given build), and combat the robotic mining mega-corp BOTNAK as they ravage the area for resources.

There are also 2 other campaigns and a collectible-littered sandbox map in the major DLC: High Seas, an aquatic map with wind and various intensity of waves, from relaxed swells to LITERAL ACTUAL TSUNAMIS; Airborne, battling against mostly aerial space pirates; and Frozen Tracks, bringing the battle back to the BOTNAK to, once again, stop them from decimating a planet in their never-ending lust for material gain.

Now, some people may not take kindly to this approach because "drowning in DLC RAAAAHHHHHH", and while I do think some of the smaller packs like Space Voyager should've just been built-in, I also recognize that they have to make money *somehow* and this game isn't exactly a mainstream AAA title so it's not like they have some massive publisher sending them truckloads of cash on a daily basis, you gotta do what you gotta do. 

In my opinion, they've earned my support because they've worked closely with their community to make an amazing game for those who have a creative itch and I'm excited to see what the next update will hold.

For those that enjoyed games like Robocraft 1&2, TeraTech, and Scrap Mechanic and wanted an exploration game with some enemies and bosses this game has been a hidden gem. I had been searching for a game that had some similarities to Robocraft and Scrap Mechanic since one is gone and the other has little to no dev communication. 

So here I am now, I've been happily surprised by the ease of entry while still having a high complexity of vehicle creation. Ranging from simple land vehicles, more complex planes, aquatic vessels & spaceships to now full on energy sword mechs.

I have thoroughly enjoyed this game since I picked it up about a year ago.

This game is genuinely more addictive then Crack Cocaine. 

I bought this game expecting a building sim, i got one that allowed me to have the power of the US government with an unchecked defense budget. The game was easy to understand, however has a learning curve for the more advanced stuff.

Trailmakers is a game all about control. Or the lack thereof.

If you're in a compaign, your tasks require to evolve a vehicle to the task at hand. This gets more difficult, the less your vehicle is grounded.
A car or tank can be created such that it can also be controlled well enough to actually complete the task. It's not easy and takes a lot of refinement (and even then has its quirks) but in general it is possible and rewarding.
Boats are already limited in some way. More so submarines, more so baloons, more so planes, MUCH more so helicopters. Hard to build, basically impossible to control well enough. You can respawn-accomplish the tasks but it never quite feels like your vehicle is up to the task. Or it would be if you could control it. But the physics engine just doesn't permit it.
This really sours the experience. Generally I love games with intricate mechanics that let me build awesome things. But if your 10th refinement iteration still produces a wobbly mess of a plane with constant down pitch and insane aiming problems due to how the camera and croisshairs work, then, well, it gets old _real_ fast.

It feels like all the physics problems of KSP times two, stacked on top of a simplified block system, stacked on top of an actual game with enemies, targets, quests, and resource limitations. It's fun in the car phase. But once the compaigns move away from cars, it keeps getting messier. In the plane phase ♥♥♥♥ really hits the fan.

5/10
Id like to start by saying I really enjoy this game as it a pretty good one. Would recommend!

Now for the parts I don't like.
Its like playing with a bunch of missing Legos, where is the 1x1?
Its great to see so many available blocks but yet its still lacking big time because of the randomness in different blocks. Again there should be a round 1x1 as well.
Theirs another game we all know of that is the exact same thing but with the ability for more formal blocks and 1x1's, why not be the same?

Lastly I don't mind the controls, there pretty good,
though there is weird snapping, your vehicle spinning in directions when entering build mode and the camera weirdly never being exactly centered. Grabbing the whole vehicle in build mode is a bad idea as it may move the whole thing across the screen or 100m away.

DLC blocks are *In My Opinion* nothing special and should've just been base game. Though I understand that the devs gotta make money to continue working on this game.

I understand these are all small things and some are just personal opinions but it'd be great to see some of these fixed.. IDK is no biggie at the end.

I LOVE THIS GAME But you probably won't

The Bad:

First of all that price tag is a LIE. The base game is practically a demo, to actually play the game in it's majority your gonna pay upwards of $45. The most essential DLCs being Airborne and High Seas. Making it to where PVP fights are practically pay to win (mainly for dogfights)

Second. This game is about a niche as it gets. You need to be very creative, you need to be good at this style of UI otherwise it can be a slog to build, you need to have a good comprehension of logic for more complex builds. Unless you are obsessed with this type of game it's not worth the money

Third. The devs are very greedy and the majority of updates are behind pay walls making you buy DLC a few times a year if you wanna keep up with the best players (PVP and design).

Forth. The campaigns are very lack luster IMO, a lot of people would disagree with my on that but I've always found them very shallow 

The Good:

You can make almost anything you can think of and it work the way you imagined. The physics system is very robust and relatively stable (compared to other engineering games with this level of realism). And with the amount of blocks in this game you can make anything including Cars, Planes, Tanks, Mechs, Boats, Submarines and even Weapons of Mass Destruction.

All types of builders have been accounted for and have there own community such as: Function builders who make mechanically complex builds, Realism builders who make the best looking builds, Logic nerds who make builds with too many features to count.

The community is still alive and well with many multiplayer servers on at any given time and players are generally non-toxic.

The PVP in this game is legendary this is the only game I know of where you can have a Dogfight, Tank battle, Race and Mech fight with builds you make from scratch that are completely destructible.

OVERALL:

I love this game and will play it for years to come but i have a hard time recommending it to most people.
